scope: First-pass survey_to_prism mapper specification for modern survey-era backtest (2008, 2012, 2016, 2020, 2024)

terminology.moral_boundary_salience: Replaces legacy 'partisan fusion' / 'tribalism' framing. Universal moral circle is the baseline; each per-boundary salience is the loading above that baseline on a specific identity-political dimension.
terminology.engagement: Continuous one-dimensional scalar (range 0-10). NOT 'activation'. Governs participation/abstention propensity.
terminology.PF_TRB: NOT mapper outputs. Legacy code may reference these field names; if so, populated as compatibility shims derived from moral-boundary outputs.

coverage_legend.strong: Standard battery directly measures the construct
coverage_legend.proxy: Adjacent measure with reasonable inferential bridge
coverage_legend.weak: No direct measure; weak inferential coverage
coverage_legend.missing: No usable variable in this source
coverage_legend.derived: Constructed from a combination of variables

uncertainty_legend.medium: 1-2 items per respondent or composite inference
uncertainty_legend.high: Sparse evidence, broad fallback prior, or structurally weak coverage

data_role.ACS: Poststratification weighting only. Never overrides survey answers for an individual respondent. May supply weak demographic priors as fallback only.
data_role.Esri: Geography hooks only. Not an individual political mapper. No PRISM target receives primary signal from Esri.

moralBoundaries.note: each per-boundary salience carries its own uncertainty flag

MAT.mapping_direction: Liberal-end answers (raise taxes, expand healthcare, support gov jobs) -> MAT position low (1-2). Free-market answers -> high (4-5).
MAT.validation_risks: Direction unambiguous. Risk: pid7 correlates strongly with MAT in modern eras — must NOT use party ID as MAT input or mapper becomes circular for vote prediction. Use party ID only for political-camp salience.

anes_year_specific: trans rights, gender identity, religious accommodation 2020+

CD.mapping_direction: Progressive answers -> CD low (1-2). Traditionalist answers -> high (4-5).
CD.uncertainty_per_respondent: medium for any 2-item CES year-specific; high for full battery
CD.validation_risks: Trans-rights items CD-loaded only 2020+. Mapper needs era-specific item selection. Mixed-position respondents need posterior smoothing.

CU.description: Cultural uniformity / pluralism (1=one shared way, 5=different ways side-by-side)

ces_year_specific: CC16_331_1-8 immigration battery (DACA, refugees, Muslim ban, English/civic)

CU.mapping_direction: Pluralist / pro-immigration / multi-culture -> CU high (4-5). Restrictionist / one-shared-way -> CU low (1-2).
CU.uncertainty_per_respondent: medium from CES year-specific; low from ANES VCF alone
CU.validation_risks: CU and CD often co-vary; joint factor analysis preferred. National-identity items load on CU AND moralBoundaries.national; mapper must split signal.

anes_cumulative: VCF0830 aid to Black Americans + immigration items + thermometer differentials

MOR_position.mapping_direction: Universalist (support refugees, foreign aid, narrow in-group/out-group thermometer gap) -> MOR high (4-5). Narrow-circle -> low (1-2).
MOR_position.uncertainty_per_respondent: medium when 3+ items; high only with thermometer-gap signal (ANES path)
MOR_position.validation_risks: MOR position and moral-boundary salience are conceptually orthogonal — narrow MOR + high overall moral-boundary loading = tribal nationalist; wide MOR + low loading = chill cosmopolitan. Mapper must not collapse these.

moralBoundaries.description: 7-boundary loading vector + intensity scalar. Replaces partisan-fusion / tribalism framing. Universal moral circle is baseline; each boundary loading is salience above baseline on that specific identity-political dimension.

source_variables: flag/anthem items in some ANES waves
national.mapping_direction: High national-pride + immigration restrictionism + 'America first' framing -> high salience (2.5-3). Globalist / anti-nationalist -> low (0-0.5).
national.uncertainty: medium typical; high when 4+ items converge
national.risks: National-pride items vary across waves; ANES more consistent than CES.

source_variables: own-race vs other-race feeling thermometer differential
ethnic_racial.mapping_direction: Wide own-race vs other-race thermometer gap + race-conscious policy stance -> high salience. Symmetric thermometers + race-blind framing -> low salience.
ethnic_racial.uncertainty: medium for CES; high for ANES with thermometers
ethnic_racial.risks: MUST NOT conflate respondent's own race with high ethnic-racial salience. Salience is about how a boundary structures political reasoning, not group membership.

source_variables: relig_imp x relig_church x relig_bornagain composite
religious.mapping_direction: High relig_imp + frequent relig_church + relig_bornagain -> high salience. Atheist/never-attends -> low (0-0.5).
religious.uncertainty: medium to high for most respondents
religious.risks: Best-covered moral boundary. Risk: cultural Christians (identify but rarely attend) may have high political religious salience that single-item relig_imp misses.

source_variables: union + union_hh + faminc x educ interaction
class.mapping_direction: Rich-vs-poor thermometer gap + economic-conflict salience + union household -> high salience. Detached-from-class-framing -> low.
class.risks: Most implicit moral boundary in US surveys. Americans rarely self-describe class identity even when their politics is class-organized. Mapper must accept low confidence.

ideological.mapping_direction: Strong ideological self-placement + wide liberal-conservative thermometer gap -> high salience. Centrist/non-ideological -> low.
ideological.risks: Strong coverage. Risk: ideology strength correlates with engagement; mapper must not double-count engagement signal.

source_variables: Me Too/Planned Parenthood thermometers (V202183, V202185 in 2020)
gender.mapping_direction: Gender-conscious policy stance + wide men-vs-women thermometer gap + Me Too engagement -> high salience. Gender-blind framing -> low.
gender.risks: Asymmetric: gender salience as a moral boundary is much higher among women in modern data, but mapper must NOT assume 'respondent is woman -> gender salience high'. Depends on whether their politics is organized around gender.

source_variables: VCF0201 - VCF0202 Dem-vs-Rep thermometer gap x VCF0301 party-ID 7-pt strength (canonical affective polarization measure)
political_camp.mapping_direction: Wide party-thermometer gap + strong partisan ID + high political-camp consciousness -> high salience. Independent/split-ticket/cross-party-warm -> low.
political_camp.uncertainty: medium for CES (pid7 strength alone is blunt); low for ANES with thermometers
political_camp.risks: ANES dramatically beats CES here. Mapper confidence much higher with thermometers. DO NOT call this 'partisan fusion' — that's a legacy framing.

intensity.computation: Composite of seven per-boundary salience values; e.g., intensity = max(per_boundary) * 0.6 + mean(per_boundary) * 0.4, capped at 3
intensity.coverage: Inherits worst coverage across contributing boundaries
intensity.uncertainty: Minimum confidence across contributing boundaries (no more reliable than weakest input)
intensity.fallback_prior: If 5+ boundaries at fallback prior, intensity also falls back to 1.0 with high uncertainty
intensity.risks: Optional output for engine consumers needing single scalar (older code paths). Should not be primary signal — per-boundary breakdown is richer.

PRO.mapping_direction: High norm-respect + procedural trust -> PRO high (4-5). Willing to break rules / 'results matter' -> low (1-2).
PRO.uncertainty_per_respondent: low to medium even with best inputs
PRO.validation_risks: Weak survey coverage. Mapper must accept PRO as highest-uncertainty continuous node. Vote-formula sensitivity to PRO is a model-coverage risk if engine relies heavily on it.

COM.mapping_direction: Pro-compromise -> COM high; principled / no-deals -> low (when items exist)

fallback_prior.salPosterior: weakly derived from pid7-strength x ideo5-strength

COM.validation_risks: Weakest-covered continuous node. v0 mapper output for COM near-uniform-prior across most of electorate. Engine consumers must know COM is not a high-signal field.

ZS.mapping_direction: 'Their gain is our loss' across trade/immigration/economic -> ZS high (4-5). Positive-sum / 'rising tide' -> ZS low (1-2).
ZS.validation_risks: ZS often correlates with moralBoundaries.national and ethnic_racial — joint estimation prevents double-counting.

ONT_H.mapping_direction: Authoritarian-parenting answers (obedience > self-reliance) -> ONT_H low (1-2; humans fixed). Permissive-parenting -> high (4-5; humans shaped by environment).
ONT_H.uncertainty_per_respondent: medium from full Feldman-Stenner battery; low otherwise
ONT_H.validation_risks: CES-only mapper path has effectively no ONT_H signal. v0 spec recommends accepting ONT_H = uniform prior for CES electorates rather than imputing from demographics (risky).

ONT_S.description: System ontology (1=rigged, 5=institutions can produce good change)

anes_year_specific: trust-in-institutions battery: gov, courts, media, science (V162310-series 2016)

ONT_S.mapping_direction: High trust in institutions + belief institutions can produce good change -> ONT_S high (4-5). 'System is rigged' / institutional nihilism -> low (1-2).
ONT_S.uncertainty_per_respondent: high for ANES path with trust battery; medium for CES
ONT_S.validation_risks: Beware partisan situational confound: under co-partisan administration, in-party partisans report higher trust than they 'really' hold. Weight non-administration-tied institutions (courts, scientists) over presidential approval.

EPS_distribution.mapping_direction: High science-trust + media-trust + journalism-trust -> empiricist or institutionalist weight. Religious-authority preference -> traditionalist. Personal-experience-only -> autonomous. Distrust all sources -> nihilist.
EPS_distribution.uncertainty_per_respondent: low to medium — categorical 6-way assignment from 2-3 items has substantial uncertainty
EPS_distribution.validation_risks: v0 mapper must output probability distribution over 6 categories, NOT hard categorical assignment. Most respondents land on a mixture rather than a clean bucket.

AES_distribution.source_variables: Surveys do not directly probe aesthetic preference
AES_distribution.validation_risks: Per ADR-003, AES is archetype-label flavor and not load-bearing for vote prediction. v0 mapper outputs uniform AES for everyone — honest answer. DO NOT infer AES from candidate-feeling-thermometer differentials (circular for vote prediction). If non-circular AES proxy identified later, this is the right place to add it.

engagement.description: Continuous one-dimensional scalar 0-10. NOT activation. Governs participation/abstention.
engagement.mapping_direction: Validated turnout=1 + newsint='most of the time' + multiple past-cycles voted + frequent political-discussion -> engagement near 10. Validated turnout=0 + newsint='hardly at all' + no past voting -> near 0.
engagement.uncertainty_per_respondent: high when vv_turnout_gvm available; medium from self-report only
engagement.explicitly_NOT: activation. Engagement is not a binary. Mapper outputs real-valued scalar — engine consumers should treat as such.
engagement.validation_risks: vv_turnout_gvm is gold-standard component; weight heavily. intent_turnout_self overstates actual turnout — discount relative to validated. Self-report bias is asymmetric (over-reporting voting much more common than under-reporting).

legacy_compatibility_shims.description: Optional output, NOT primary mapper concepts. Sunset when engine no longer reads these field names directly.

PF_legacy.derivation: posPosterior reflecting moralBoundaries.political_camp.salience mapped to 1-5 scale (salience 0 -> pos 1; salience 3 -> pos 5)

TRB_legacy.derivation: posPosterior reflecting moralBoundaries.intensity x dominant_boundary_indicator mapped to 1-5

five_way_failure_classification: mapper_error: survey-to-PRISM mapping miscalibrated for the failing subgroup. Revise this spec.
candidate_signature_error: candidate's PRISM coding off. Revise candidate signature, NOT mapper.
turnout_engagement_error: mapper's engagement mis-estimates participation. Investigate engagement coverage first; only revise mapper if engagement inputs clearly under-utilized.
vote_formula_error: engine's distance/weighting/clearing-bar logic produces wrong outputs even with correct mapper outputs. Engine-side issue; mapper unchanged.
benchmark_or_coverage_error: benchmark missing, subgroup tag mis-spelled, or population not in scope. Fix benchmarks/tagger; mapper unchanged.
mapper_error_decision_rules: Candidate signature for the election independently audited and reasonable
Engagement/turnout for failing subgroup tracks reported levels (no gross mis-estimation)
Engine vote-formula validated against other-cycle predictions for similar subgroups
Failing subgroup's per-target uncertainty was NOT already flagged 'high' (high-uncertainty targets are expected to mispredict; that's coverage gap, not mapper bug)
no_overfit_guardrails: One-cycle rule: do NOT revise mapping based on a single year's failure. Hold proposed change against >=2 cycles. If improves failing year but worsens another by >=1 severity bucket, reject.
Subgroup-symmetric check: a revision helping one subgroup at cost of another is usually a coverage problem, not a mapper problem.
Direction-of-effect sanity: revision must have defensible theoretical direction-of-effect, not just empirical one.
Document every revision with: failing year, failing subgroup, gate-output severity, hypothesized cause, proposed change, cross-cycle holdout result, decision (accept/reject/per-cycle override).

structural_vs_per_cycle.structural_revision: Changes mapping for all cycles equally. Requires holdout validation across >=3 cycles + revision history entry.
structural_vs_per_cycle.per_cycle_override: Documented adjustment for a specific year (e.g., Vietnam-elevated national salience in 1968). Each override is a coverage gap acknowledged, not a fix — flagged as known gap until structural revision absorbs it.

never_trigger_revision: A single year's popular-vote-share miss without coalition decomposition
Pressure from engine knob debates (different problem)
An election that surprised pundits (an election surprising pundits is not, by itself, evidence the mapper is wrong)

open_issues_for_v1: Joint vs independent posterior fitting — v0 treats targets independently; v1 should explore joint fitting (e.g., MAT and CD have known joint structure: libertarians, populists)
morBoundaries inference order — parallel vs sequential boundary fitting; v0 treats as parallel, v1 should test
Engagement -> turnout function — engine-side decision; mapper's 0-10 scale calibrated to be easy to map to clearing-bar thresholds
Era-specific item availability — v0 coverage labels assume modern CES/ANES; pre-2008 mappings need different (mostly thinner) labels
ANES VCF code verification — codes from training-data knowledge in A1 should be verified against actual codebooks before any ANES-VCF mapping is coded
